scenario: RPA bot extracts text from PDF documents via OCR. Wonders integration.
question: RPA OCR / document extraction?

options.text: md) modes. md:26, NOT generic Python); call Tesseract/PaddleOCR keywords from Robot. md; Google Vision / Azure Form Recognizer via REST Connector. - Workflow: IDP Service Task (for structured) → result variables OR RPA Task (for UI-heavy + OCR) → variables. - For complex docs: IDP > generic RPA OCR; cloud OCR > Tesseract for accuracy.

Camunda 8 has no OCR or document extraction capabilities -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. Camunda IDP (Intelligent Document Processing) offers built-in LLM-driven extraction for structured business documents like invoices and contracts. The RPA subsystem supports Robot Framework scripts that can call OCR libraries such as Tesseract or PaddleOCR. The OOTB connector catalog includes the AWS Textract Connector for cloud-based OCR. The REST Connector can call Google Vision or Azure Form Recognizer APIs.

While AWS Textract, Google Vision, and Azure Form Recognizer are valid options through connectors, Camunda 8 also supports local OCR through the RPA subsystem. Robot Framework scripts running as RPA tasks can use OCR libraries like Tesseract installed locally on the bot runner. Camunda IDP provides another built-in option. The choice depends on accuracy requirements, cost constraints, and whether cloud API access is acceptable for the document content.

Manual human transcription of PDF content is the only option in Camunda 8 -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. Automating PDF text extraction is one of the primary use cases for Camunda 8's RPA and document processing capabilities. Camunda IDP handles structured document extraction automatically, RPA bots can extract text using OCR libraries, and cloud OCR connectors (AWS Textract, Google Vision) provide high-accuracy automated extraction. Manual transcription would be appropriate only for extremely specialized edge cases not suitable for automation.

a.text: Correct. OCR options: Approach 1: Camunda IDP (per Set 13 Q15): Intelligent Document Processing. Built for: structured document extraction. Auto-extracts: fields from invoices, receipts, etc. Schema-based: define expected fields. Higher-level than raw OCR. Best for: Business documents: invoices, contracts, forms. Structured extraction: specific fields. Approach 2: RPA with OCR library: Bot script uses OCR lib (Tesseract, etc.). Bot reads PDF: extracts text. Returns: text + parsed fields. For Robot Framework + Tesseract (Pattern #19 -- C8 RPA = Robot Framework, NOT Python; Tesseract called via OCRLibrary keyword): `robotframework Settings Library OCRLibrary Tasks Extract Invoice ${text}= Read Image invoice.pdf Set Output Variable extractedText ${text} Best for: Unstructured text extraction. When IDP overkill. Approach 3: External OCR services: AWS Textract / Google Vision / Azure Form Recognizer. Cloud OCR: high accuracy. REST Connector: call from BPMN. Higher cost: per request. Approach 4: Custom Connector with OCR library: Custom Connector wraps OCR library. Bundled deployment. Cost-controlled. Comparison: | Approach | Use case | Cost | Accuracy | |----------|----------|------|----------| | IDP | Business docs | Camunda licensing | High for structured | | RPA + OCR | Mixed UI / docs | Bot infrastructure | Variable | | External OCR | Cloud service | Per-request | Very high | | Custom Connector | Custom needs | Self-managed | Depends on lib | For the scenario (PDF text extraction): Pick based on: Structured business doc: IDP. Mixed UI navigation + extraction: RPA + OCR. High accuracy / complex: external service (Textract, etc.). Workflow examples: RPA + OCR: [RPA Task: extract PDF text] bot script: Open PDF (Adobe Reader or library) Capture screen / extract text OCR if image-based Return text + fields IDP: [IDP Service Task] Submit PDF Receive structured fields Process External OCR: [Service Task: Upload to OCR API] POST PDF Receive extracted text + fields Process ` For accuracy comparison: IDP: tuned for business docs; very accurate on structured. Tesseract: free; good general; weaker on poor scans. Cloud OCR: very accurate; ML-based; cost. For preprocessing: Image quality: critical for OCR. Bot: enhance (rotate, contrast, denoise). Then OCR. For confidence scoring: OCR outputs confidence: per word / field. Reject low-confidence: route to human review. For combining: Hybrid: IDP for structured fields. OCR for free-text portions. Combined into single output. For Camunda 8 integration: IDP: typically built-in. RPA bots: full Camunda RPA. Connectors: REST to cloud OCR. For local OCR libraries: Tesseract: open-source; widely used. PaddleOCR: better for some languages. Each has trade-offs. For PDF processing: Text PDFs (text layer): Direct extraction: no OCR needed. Libraries: PyPDF2, Apache PDFBox. Image PDFs (scanned): OCR required. Mixed: Detect type: per page. Process accordingly. For language support: OCR languages: install dictionaries. Multi-language: detect or specify. For handwriting: Standard OCR: poor. Specialized: cloud services. For tables in documents: Specialised table extraction: needed. Tabula, Camelot for PDF tables. Cloud services: built-in. For audit + compliance: Original docs preserved: not just extracted text. Audit trail: extraction process. Manual review: for low-confidence. For privacy / PII: Sensitive in docs: handle carefully. GDPR considerations: storage + processing. For storage: Original docs: Document Handling / S3. Extracted text: process variable or external. Performance: OCR slow: seconds to minutes per doc. Cloud services faster. Bound activation timeout accordingly. For batch processing: MI subprocess: per document. Parallel extraction: bulk. For testing: Sample documents: known content. Verify extraction accuracy. Best practices: Use IDP for structured business docs. RPA + OCR for mixed scenarios. External cloud for high accuracy. Preprocess for quality. Confidence-based routing. Anti-patterns: Generic OCR for structured business docs: IDP better. No preprocessing: poor results. No confidence threshold: bad data flows downstream. For the scenario (PDF text extraction): First choice: IDP for business docs. Or external cloud OCR: high accuracy. RPA + Tesseract: budget-conscious. For implementation choice: Based on: Document type (structured vs unstructured). Accuracy requirements. Budget. Existing infrastructure.
